# @mongez/config

> A tiny, framework-agnostic configuration tree. Drop a tree of values in once at boot; read them anywhere by dotted path with a default fallback. Three methods: `config.set`, `config.get`, `config.list`. No dependencies beyond `@mongez/reinforcements`.

This is the configuration primitive of the Mongez family. For reactive state (subscriptions, derived values, SSR isolation) see [`@mongez/atom`](https://github.com/hassanzohdy/atom).

The default export is a singleton — all importers share one tree.

## Docs

- [Overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/skills/overview.md): Pitch, install, mental model.
  - *Auto-trigger:* import default `config` or `ConfigurationsList` from `@mongez/config`, or when the user needs orientation to the singleton and its three methods.
- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/README.md): Marketing-style index.
- [Changelog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/CHANGELOG.md): Release notes.

## Reference (by feature)

- [Reading](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/skills/reading.md): `config.get(path, default?)`. Dot-notation, numeric array indices, default-fallback behavior on missing keys vs. falsy values.
  - *Auto-trigger:* code calls `config.get` or the user asks about reading values, dot-notation paths, or default-vs-falsy fallback behaviour.
- [Writing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/skills/writing.md): `config.set(treeOrPath, value?)`. Object-form deep merge vs. path-form per-key write. Intermediate object/array creation.
  - *Auto-trigger:* code calls `config.set` or the user asks about deep-merging, path-form writes, or array-replacement behaviour.
- [Listing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/skills/listing.md): `config.list()`. Live-reference semantics and snapshot pitfalls.
  - *Auto-trigger:* code calls `config.list` or the user asks about snapshotting, cloning, or debug-dumping the whole config tree.
- [Typing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/skills/typing.md): `ConfigurationsList`, project-specific shapes, typed read helpers.
  - *Auto-trigger:* code imports `ConfigurationsList`, calls `config.get<T>(...)`, or the user asks about type-safe config reads or `AppConfig` shapes.

## Recipes

- [Recipes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/skills/recipes.md): Multi-source boot, env-var overrides, feature flags, reactive config on top of `@mongez/atom`.
  - *Auto-trigger:* multiple chained `config.set` boot calls, or the user asks about feature flags, env-driven config, or reactivity via `@mongez/atom`.

## Quick rules

1. `config.set(obj)` is a **deep merge** — it does not replace the tree. Use it for layered configuration (base + env + overrides).
2. `config.set(path, value)` writes to a single dotted path. Intermediate containers are created on demand (objects, or arrays when the next segment is a numeric index).
3. `config.get(path, default)` returns `default` only when the path is missing or terminates in `undefined`. Other falsy values (`0`, `""`, `false`, `null`) pass through.
4. The default for the second argument of `get` is `null`, not `undefined`.
5. `config.list()` returns the **live** tree by reference — mutating the result mutates the config. Clone for snapshots.
6. Keys that contain literal dots are unreachable via `get` — paths are split on `.`. Avoid `"api.example.com"` as a single key.
7. The config is a process-wide singleton. For per-request isolation (SSR), use `@mongez/atom`'s `AtomStore`, not this package.

## Optional

- [Full single-file reference (llms-full.txt)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config/main/llms-full.txt): All reference content concatenated.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-config): Source code, issues, releases.
